channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced Appears on Ubisoft Launcher
item.content:encoded:
It seems that Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Remake (aka Resynced) could actually be revealed today, as a listing has appeared on the Ubisoft launcher.

A report by Insider Gaming earlier this month stated that an announcement had been planned for April 16th for some time, with mentions of ‘mid-April’ appearing repeatedly. However, that is yet to be officially confirmed, but the new listing might suggest that the information is indeed true.

Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced Ubisoft Listing Shows Edward In a Brand New Outfit

Spotted on r/GamingLeaksAndRumours, a listing for Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced appeared on the Ubisoft launcher today but has since been taken down. However, players were quick enough to grab a screenshot, and this is what we learned. The poster features Edward Kenway in a brand new black and gold outfit, and the description states, “Set sail for the Caribbean. (Re)play this classic swashbucking adventure on Day 1 with Ubisoft+ Premium.”

Following Ubisoft’s announcement of a restructuring, many were concerned that the remake of Assassin’s Creed Black Flag might face the same fate as the Prince of Persia remake. However, the developers have confirmed that the game is indeed in development. Despite this confirmation, we still lack official information on when and where Ubisoft plans to reveal the game. However, if a listing has surfaced, the reveal could be just around the corner. We will have to wait for an official announcement to know for sure.

In other news, the rating leak for Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced has confirmed the existence of new content for the upcoming remake. Additionally, the developer of Dawnfolk recently shared his experience leaving what he thought was his dream job at Ubisoft.

What are your thoughts on Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced appearing on Ubisoft’s launcher? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Battlefield 6’s roadmap being leaked ahead of the official reveal. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Battlefield 6 2026 Roadmap Leaks Ahead of Official Reveal
item.content:encoded:
Battlefield 6’s 2026 roadmap has leaked, revealing the new maps, features, and more coming to the game across Seasons 3, 4, and 5.

There are still plenty of days to go before Battlefield 6 Season 3 arrives, as Season 2 will go on till May 12. Ahead of its launch, we have some idea of the maps and features that will be introduced in the game’s new seasonal update. Now, a leak reveals content for the next few seasons.

Battlefield 6 Will Get 2 Maps Each for the Next 2 Seasons, and 3 Maps in Season 5

The latest leak for Battlefield 6’s 2026 roadmap comes from onebattlefield’s X account (formerly Twitter). The leakers obtained information from the presentation before the embargo was lifted and have revealed all the new content planned for the next three seasons.

Starting with Battlefield 6 Season 3, players will seemingly receive 2 new maps: Railway to Golmud and Cairo Bazar. Season 4 will see the arrival of two more maps, Tsuru Reef and Wake Island. Lastly, Season 5 will bring three new maps; however, that information is classified. The new maps, modes, and content are currently being tested in Battlefield Labs.

Battlefield 6 Prioritizing Features Like Proximity Chat, Platoons, Server Browser, and More

Along with the maps, the Battlefield 6 roadmap for 2026 also revealed some upcoming features. BF Season 3 will see the arrival of BR Solos, BR Ranked Play & Leaderboard, new weapons, modes, and more. Season 4 will bring Naval Warfare, Custom Lobbies, Spectator Mode, new weapons, modes, and more. Season 5’s information is classified.

Apart from these, the features in the priority list include Proximity Chat, Platoons, Server Browser, and MP leaderboards. They have also planned quality-of-life improvements such as combat tuning, soldier visibility, matchmaking, and challenges & progression. The embargo on this information is expected to lift at 15:00 UTC+0 today.

In other news, OpTic Gaming’s pro players have bagged on Battlefield 6, suggesting it’s a slow game for dad gamers. Additionally, Electronic Arts utilized AI tools to accelerate the production of Battlefield 6. What are your thoughts on the leaked Battlefield 6 2026 roadmap? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about a new claim about PlayStation 6’s backward compatibility. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.
